metadata.title: ICH CMC Analytical Methods, Impurity Limits, Specifications & GMP Rules
sources: ICH Q3A(R2) — Impurities in New Drug Substances
ICH Q3B(R2) — Impurities in New Drug Products
ICH Q7 — GMP for Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients
metadata.coverage: 87 PDF files across ICH Q2, Q3, Q4, Q6, Q7, Q14 guideline families

approaches.enhanced: Systematic approach using prior knowledge, risk assessment, DoE, multivariate analysis to identify proven acceptable ranges (PARs)

stage_2_validation.purpose: Confirm procedure is fit for intended purpose

requirements: Trend analysis of control samples, SST data, reference material results
Statistical process control (control charts)
Periodic verification against reference procedure at justified intervals
Revalidation triggered by changes, new degradation products, matrix changes, or adverse trends

analytical_target_profile.definition: Prospective summary of performance characteristics describing intended purpose and anticipated performance criteria for the reportable result
required_elements: Link to the CQA and its specification range
Performance characteristics with acceptance criteria (accuracy, precision, specificity, reportable range)
analytical_target_profile.key_property: Technology-independent — multiple technologies may satisfy the same ATP

potency_bioassay.specificity: Specific for intended mechanism of action

nir_assay.specificity: API unique spectral features in PLS loadings

specificity.definition: Ability to unequivocally assess the analyte in presence of expected components
methods: Spiking studies at relevant impurity levels
Forced degradation studies (heat, light, humidity, acid/base, oxidation)

special_requirements.biological_assays: Demonstrate specificity for intended mechanism of action; no dose-response from cell line alone
special_requirements.qPCR: Orthogonal comparison (gel electrophoresis, melting profile, DNA sequencing); BLAST primer/probe specificity
special_requirements.multivariate_NIR_Raman: Spectral overlay of DS/tablet/placebo; loading plot comparison; outlier rejection

repeatability.minimum_determinations: 6 at 100% test concentration OR 3 levels × 3 replicates = 9 total
repeatability.biological_assays: ≥3 replicates at ≥5 levels covering reportable range
repeatability.qPCR: ≥5 DNA concentration levels, ≥3 replicates each, single assay

intermediate_precision.biological_assays: 6 independent determinations at 3 levels, 2 analysts, 3 days

reproducibility.when_required: Method transfer, collaborative studies; not routinely required for registration

methods: Comparison to reference standard or CRM
Inferred from precision + specificity + linearity (especially bioassays)

reportable_range.definition: Interval between upper and lower concentration with suitable accuracy, precision, and response function
reportable_range.note: Replaces former separate 'linearity' and 'range' concepts from Q2(R1)

nonlinear.requirement: Demonstrate and justify the calibration model

detection_limit.definition: Lowest amount detectable but not necessarily quantitated
detection_limit.when_required: Limit tests on impurities; informational for other tests

quantitation_limit.definition: Lowest amount quantitatively determined with suitable accuracy and precision
quantitation_limit.verification: Must demonstrate acceptable accuracy and precision at QL level
quantitation_limit.when_required: Quantitative impurity tests; defines lower boundary of reportable range

robustness.definition: Capacity to remain unaffected by small deliberate parameter variations

bioassay.dose_response_curve: Sigmoid with upper and lower plateaus
bioassay.parallelism_ratios: 0.8–1.2 for asymptote and Hill slope ratios

biological_assay_criteria.note: Use geometric coefficient of variation (%GCV) with log transformation

intermediate_precision.design: 6 independent determinations at 3 levels, 2 analysts, 3 days

identification_thresholds.threshold: 1.0% or 5 µg TDI, whichever is lower
0.5% or 20 µg TDI, whichever is lower
0.2% or 2 mg TDI, whichever is lower

qualification_thresholds.threshold: 1.0% or 50 µg TDI, whichever is lower
0.5% or 200 µg TDI, whichever is lower
0.2% or 3 mg TDI, whichever is lower

reporting_format: Each specified unidentified impurity (by qualitative descriptor)
Any unspecified impurity with a limit

identification_thresholds.threshold: 1.0% or 5 µg TDI, whichever is lower
0.5% or 20 µg TDI, whichever is lower
0.2% or 2 mg TDI, whichever is lower

qualification_thresholds.threshold: 1.0% or 50 µg TDI, whichever is lower
0.5% or 200 µg TDI, whichever is lower
0.5% or 200 µg TDI, whichever is lower
0.2% or 3 mg TDI, whichever is lower

class_1_avoid.description: Known human carcinogens, strongly suspected carcinogens, environmental hazards

class_2_limit.description: Non-genotoxic animal carcinogens or possible causative agents of irreversible toxicity

class_3_low_toxicity.description: PDE ≥50 mg/day; 5000 ppm acceptable without justification

calculation_options.option_1: Concentration limits in ppm (assumes max 10 g/day dose)
calculation_options.option_2: PDE-based: concentration (ppm) = (1000 × PDE) / actual daily dose

PDE_derivation.formula: PDE = (NOEL × body_weight) / (F1 × F2 × F3 × F4 × F5)

class_1.description: Human toxicants with limited/no pharmaceutical use; evaluate across ALL routes

class_2A.description: High probability of occurrence; risk assessment across ALL routes

class_2B.description: Lower probability; include only if intentionally added or identified as risk

class_3.description: Relatively low oral toxicity (oral PDE >500 µg/day); evaluate only if intentionally added

assessment_options.option_1: Compare to permitted concentrations; assumes daily dose ≤10 g
assessment_options.option_2a: Apply PDE to drug product; measure in product × daily dose
assessment_options.option_2b: Apply PDE across all components; sum DS + excipients + manufacturing + CCS

Q3D_elemental_impurities.parenteral_PDE_derivation: When no parenteral data: divide oral PDE by factor of 10

Q3E_extractables_leachables.scope: Packaging/delivery systems and manufacturing equipment for all dosage forms

analytical_evaluation_threshold.description: Threshold above which E&L should be identified, quantified, and reported
analytical_evaluation_threshold.formula: AET = SCT × UF × dosing conversion factors
analytical_evaluation_threshold.note: AET is drug product-specific, not a control threshold

Q3E_extractables_leachables.safety_concern_threshold: SCT = lower of TTC and QT

class_1_avoid.description: ICH M7 cohort of concern, Class 1 with AI <1.5 µg/day, non-mutagenic with low parenteral PDE
class_1_avoid.action: Avoid; if unavoidable, compound-specific safety limit required

class_2_limit.description: Default classification; mutagens with AI ≥1.5 µg/day; non-mutagens with parenteral PDE >QT
class_2_limit.action: Should not exceed TTC or QT

class_3_low_toxicity.description: Non-mutagenic leachables with chronic parenteral PDE ≥1 mg/day
class_3_low_toxicity.action: Qualified up to 1.0 mg/day without further safety justification

universal_tests.description: Qualitative statement of state and color
universal_tests.identification: Minimum 2 tests; one by IR spectroscopy recommended; second from different technique
universal_tests.assay: Specific, stability-indicating procedure; titration acceptable if justified

specific_tests: Physicochemical properties (melting point, refractive index, optical rotation)
Particle size (when critical per Decision Tree #3)
Polymorphic forms (when affecting safety/performance/efficacy per Decision Tree #4)
Water content (Karl Fischer or loss on drying)

universal_tests.description: Shape, size, color, markings, scoring; clarity/color for solutions
universal_tests.assay: Specific, stability-indicating; content as % label claim

decision_trees.DT1_specified_impurity_acceptance: Mean batch level + upper confidence limit (3×SD); add stability increase if degradation product; compare to qualified level
decision_trees.DT2_degradation_product_acceptance: DS level + manufacturing increase + shelf-life increase; compare to qualified level
decision_trees.DT3_particle_size: Required if critical to dissolution, bioavailability, processability, stability, content uniformity, or appearance
decision_trees.DT4_polymorphism: Screen → characterize forms → assess impact on safety/performance/efficacy → set acceptance criteria if impacted
decision_trees.DT5_chirality: Single enantiomer: chiral identity + chiral assay + enantiomeric impurity testing
decision_trees.DT6_microbial_quality: Based on growth support capability; skip-lot testing if consistently below limits

DT7_dissolution.part1: Modified release → multi-point; High solubility + rapid dissolution + disintegration correlation → disintegration test acceptable; Otherwise → single-point dissolution
DT7_dissolution.part2: If dissolution affects bioavailability → discriminating conditions; otherwise → non-discriminating acceptable
DT7_dissolution.part3: IVIVC → acceptance ranges from correlation; Extended release ranges >20% of label → provide bioequivalence data

decision_trees.DT8_microbial_drug_product: Preservative-containing → chemical acceptance criteria + effectiveness testing; Dry dosage forms → may not need testing if growth-inhibitory

dissolution_criteria.rapid_dissolution_definition: >80% dissolved in 15 min at pH 1.2, 4.0, and 6.8
dissolution_criteria.high_solubility_definition: Dose/solubility volume ≤250 mL across pH 1.2–6.8 at 37±0.5°C

Q6B_biotech_biological.scope: Proteins and polypeptides from recombinant or non-recombinant cell culture

characterization.physicochemical: Composition, physical properties, primary structure, higher-order structure inferred from biological activity

biological_activity.potency_expression: Units calibrated against international/national reference standard
biological_activity.replacement_by_physicochemical: Only when (a) sufficient physicochemical + higher-order structure correlation to activity demonstrated AND (b) well-established manufacturing history

characterization.immunochemical: Binding, affinity, avidity, immunoreactivity, cross-reactivity (antibody products)

product_related.note: Product-related SUBSTANCES (active variants from anticipated post-translational modification) are NOT impurities

contaminants.description: Adventitiously introduced; must be strictly avoided
contaminants.viral: Action limits not applicable; refer to ICH Q5A

reference_standards.requirement: In-house primary reference material at time of submission
reference_standards.documentation: Characterization, storage conditions, and stability data required

structural_characterization_methods: Terminal amino acid analysis (N- and C-terminal)
Peptide mapping (enzymatic/chemical fragmentation, HPLC, MS identification)
Sulfhydryl groups and disulfide bridge mapping
Carbohydrate structure (neutral sugars, amino sugars, sialic acids, oligosaccharide pattern, glycosylation sites)

framework.definition: Official texts from JP, EP, USP can be substituted one for another for pharmaceutical registration in ICH regions
framework.principle: An analyst reaches the same accept/reject decision irrespective of which PDG pharmacopoeia is used

conditions: Sample weight justified if not in monograph
Muffle furnace calibrated per regional GMP
NOT interchangeable at 100 mL nominal volume (JP more stringent)
Media and disc use specified in dossier
Mass variation NOT interchangeable unless 25 mg/25% threshold met
FDA rejects 2% RSD exception in JP/EP
NOT interchangeable with enzymes in media
Batches >500 containers of ≥100 mL: minimum 20 or 2% of containers, whichever lower
Gel-clot limit test for dispute resolution
Reference standards calibrated against WHO International Standard

harmonized_numerical_limits.tablet_friability: NMT 1.0% mass loss (single or mean of 3 determinations)

particulate_contamination_system_suitabi.particle_free_water: NMT 5 particles ≥10µm, NMT 2 particles ≥25µm in 10 mL

regional_notes.EU: Ph. Eur. has MANDATORY applicability; use of another pharmacopoeia accepted as fulfilling Ph. Eur. requirements
regional_notes.FDA: May request demonstration that chosen method is suitable for specific material/product; rejects 2% RSD exception for Uniformity
regional_notes.MHLW: Accepts per annex conditions via Ministerial Notification
regional_notes.Health_Canada: Added via R1 revisions (2010) to Annexes 1-10; from inception for 11-14

quality_unit.independence: Person responsible for final quality decisions must not have production responsibilities
responsibilities: Release/reject all APIs, intermediates, raw materials
Review completed batch production and lab records of critical steps
Approve specifications, master production instructions, all quality-impacting procedures
Ensure stability data supports retest/expiry dates

contents: Critical IPC and API test results

water.sterile_API_final_steps: Monitor for microbial counts, objectionable organisms, and endotoxins

facilities.cross_contamination: No highly toxic non-pharmaceutical materials in API buildings

requirements: Directed to situations posing greatest risk to API quality
Protocol: equipment, procedures, materials, acceptable levels, parameters, methods, sample types
Residue limits: practical, achievable, verifiable, based on most deleterious residue
Validated analytical methods sensitive to detect residues at acceptable levels
Maximum dirty hold time established and confirmed
Microbiological and endotoxin contamination addressed where relevant

calibration.non_conforming: Must not be used; investigate impact since last successful calibration

record_retention.with_expiry_date: At least 1 year after batch expiry
record_retention.with_retest_date: At least 3 years after batch completely distributed
record_retention.entries: Made indelibly, directly after performing activities, identify person

master_production_instructions.approval: Prepared, dated, signed; independently checked by quality unit
contents: Complete raw material list with quantities

production_controls.critical_operations: Witnessed or subjected to equivalent control
production_controls.time_limits: Must be met; deviations documented and evaluated

in_process_controls.acceptance_criteria: Based on development or historical data
in_process_controls.stringency: Less stringent early steps; tighter later steps (isolation, purification)
in_process_controls.adjustments: Allowed within pre-established limits approved by quality unit

rules: OOS batches must NOT be blended
Each batch individually tested before blending

specifications.microbiological: Action limits for total microbial counts and objectionable organisms

impurity_profile.comparison: At appropriate intervals against historical data

laboratory_controls.OOS_investigation: Documented procedure; analysis of data, problem assessment, corrective actions

contents: Each test performed with acceptance limits
Date, signature of authorized quality unit

stability_program.ongoing: At least 1 batch per year, tested annually
stability_program.short_shelf_life: ≤1 year: monthly for first 3 months, then 3-month intervals

reserve_samples.with_expiry: Retained 1 year after expiry OR 3 years after distribution, whichever longer
reserve_samples.with_retest: Retained 3 years after batch completely distributed
reserve_samples.quantity: Sufficient for at least 2 full compendial/specification analyses
reserve_samples.packaging: Same or more protective than marketed

process_validation.prospective: 3 consecutive successful batches (guide); completed before commercial distribution
process_validation.concurrent: When limited batches available or infrequent production
process_validation.retrospective: 10–30 consecutive batches examined; only for well-established processes

validation.analytical_method_validation: Per ICH Q2; suitability verified under actual conditions regardless of source

requirements: Formal system for all changes affecting production and control
Written procedures for identification, documentation, review, approval
Quality impact evaluation
Classification as minor or major
First batches after change evaluated
Impact on retest/expiry dates evaluated
Dosage form manufacturers notified of quality-impacting changes

rejection_reuse.reprocessing: Repeating established steps (crystallization, distillation, filtration, chromatography, milling) generally acceptable
rejection_reuse.reworking: Different steps from established process; requires investigation, testing, stability testing, impurity profile comparison, concurrent validation
rejection_reuse.solvent_recovery: Acceptable if procedures controlled/monitored and recovered materials meet specifications
rejection_reuse.returns: Quarantine; if storage/shipping in doubt, reprocess/rework/destroy

agents_brokers_distributors.applicability: ALL parties after original manufacturer to drug product manufacturer
agents_brokers_distributors.repackaging: Under appropriate GMP; stability studies if different container type
agents_brokers_distributors.label_rules: Distributors should NOT remove original labels; may ADD additional labels

cell_bank.storage: Conditions to maintain viability and prevent contamination

requirements: Aseptic addition in closed/contained systems
Biosafety cabinet for sensitive manipulations
Critical parameters monitored (temperature, pH, agitation, gases, pressure)
Equipment cleaned and sterilized after use
Media sterilized before use
cell_culture.contamination: Detect, assess impact, decontaminate; foreign organisms assessed for product quality

requirements: Critical steps performed within validated parameters
Pre-viral to post-viral contamination prevention
Open processing in separate areas with separate air handling
Same equipment not normally used for different purification steps

clinical_trial_APIs.controls: Consistent with development stage; flexible procedures allowed
clinical_trial_APIs.validation: Process validation normally inappropriate at clinical stage; equipment qualification required
clinical_trial_APIs.analytical_methods: May not yet be validated but must be scientifically sound
clinical_trial_APIs.reserve_samples: Retained for appropriate time after approval, termination, or discontinuation

established_conditions.PA_prior_approval: Changes to high-risk ECs (ATP performance criteria, analytical procedure principle change)
established_conditions.NM_notification_moderate: Changes within same technology principle, SST elements
established_conditions.NL_notification_low: Cell preparation changes, medium composition, low-risk parameters controlled by SST
established_conditions.not_reported: Non-EC parameters with no impact based on development data

requirements: Full validation of new procedure
Comparative analysis of representative samples and reference materials
Demonstrate comparable accept/reject discrimination
Partial or full revalidation of affected characteristics
Comparative analysis as appropriate

low_risk.description: Transfer to different site, no procedure change
requirements: Partial/full revalidation AND/OR comparative analysis
Or justification for not performing transfer experiments
